title: Conjecture 1.1 (Arman, Gunderson and Tsaturian)
statement_text: For any $k > 1$, for sufficiently large $n$, $T_2(n)$ is the unique $n$-vertex $C_{2k+1}$-free graph containing $m(n; C_{2k+1})$ cycles.
context_text: Durocher, Gunderson, Li and Skala conjectured that $m(n, K_3)$ is attained by $T_2(n)$; this was proved by Arman, Gunderson and Tsaturian, who then posed this generalisation to odd-cycle-free graphs. A partial result $m(n; C_{2k+1}) = O(c(T_2(n)))$ was established in the same paper.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for any fixed $k$ and all sufficiently large $n$ as a special case of the more general Theorem 1.3, using the fact that an odd cycle contains a critical edge.
paper_title: Maximising the Number of Cycles in Graphs with Forbidden Subgraphs

title: Question 1.2 (Arman, Gunderson and Tsaturian)
statement_text: For $k \geq 4$, what is $m(n; K_k)$? Is $T_{k-1}(n)$ the $K_k$-free graph containing $m(n; K_k)$ cycles?
context_text: After resolving the triangle-free case, Arman, Gunderson and Tsaturian asked about the natural generalisation of maximising cycles in complete-graph-free graphs, proposing the Turán graph $T_{k-1}(n)$ as the extremal graph for $K_k$-free graphs.
paper_contribution: Answers the question affirmatively for all sufficiently large $n$ via Theorem 1.3, since $K_k$ has chromatic number $k$ and contains a critical edge.
paper_title: Maximising the Number of Cycles in Graphs with Forbidden Subgraphs
